_comment: Cross-binding conformance reference for interp.linear (esm-spec §9.2). Each `success` scenario gives positional inputs [table, axis, x] and the expected scalar. Tolerance is 0/0 because the chosen inputs are exactly representable in IEEE-754 binary64 and the spec-pinned evaluation order (`result = t[i] + w * (t[i+1] - t[i])`) produces the same Float64 result on any conformant binding. Bindings that selectively use FMA MUST verify their results still match these references; if a binding cannot meet 0/0 on a particular fixture, raise the issue against esm-94w rather than relaxing the fixture silently. Out-of-range queries clamp to the nearest endpoint (extrapolate-flat).

scenarios.name: exact_at_interior_knot
scenarios.description: x lands exactly on an interior axis knot — return the corresponding table entry without blending.

scenarios.name: exact_at_last_knot
scenarios.description: x = axis[N] returns table[N] exactly. With the pinned form `t[i] + w*(t[i+1]-t[i])` and w handled by clamp, no rounding error from (1-w) cancellation.

scenarios.name: midpoint_first_cell
scenarios.description: x at midpoint of [axis[1], axis[2]] = 0.5; w = 0.5; result = 10 + 0.5*(20-10) = 15.

scenarios.name: midpoint_interior_cell
scenarios.description: x at midpoint of [axis[2], axis[3]] = 1.5; w = 0.5; result = 20 + 0.5*(40-20) = 30.

scenarios.name: nan_x_returns_nan
scenarios.description: NaN x propagates through the blend (clamp comparisons return false for NaN, falling through to step 3 where the weight is NaN). Expected output is NaN.

scenarios.name: two_point_table_below_clamps
scenarios.description: Minimal-size table; x below axis[1] returns table[1].

scenarios.name: two_point_table_above_clamps
scenarios.description: Minimal-size table; x above axis[N] returns table[N].

scenarios.name: non_uniform_axis_blend
scenarios.description: Axis spacing varies. x = 3.0 in cell [1, 5]; w = (3-1)/(5-1) = 0.5; result = 1 + 0.5*(4-1) = 2.5.

scenarios.name: non_uniform_axis_at_interior_knot
scenarios.description: Axis spacing varies. x lands on an interior knot.

scenarios.name: negative_axis_and_table_values
scenarios.description: Both axis and table take negative values; x in interior cell. axis=[-2, -1, 0, 1], table=[-10, -5, 0, 5]; x=-0.5, w=0.5, result = -5 + 0.5*(0-(-5)) = -2.5.

error_scenarios.name: non_monotonic_axis
error_scenarios.description: Axis decreases at one position. Bindings MUST reject at load time with diagnostic code `interp_non_monotonic_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: equal_adjacent_axis
error_scenarios.description: Axis has two equal-adjacent entries (denominator zero in blend). Bindings MUST reject with `interp_non_monotonic_axis` (`interp.linear` requires strictly increasing, unlike `interp.searchsorted` which permits non-decreasing).

error_scenarios.name: axis_table_length_mismatch
error_scenarios.description: table has 4 entries, axis has 3. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_length_mismatch`.

error_scenarios.name: nan_in_axis
error_scenarios.description: Axis contains a NaN entry. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_nan_in_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: axis_too_short
error_scenarios.description: Axis has only one entry (no interval to blend). Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_too_short`.
